old_claim.note: Insurance Brokerage Offices provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Bills of exchange and related credit instruments were part of the long-distance finance environment recorded in merchant ledgers, but were not physical ledger components.

source_shape.source_locator: Merchant-account-book section: Barbarigo used bills of exchange to move funds, relied on bankers for loans and overdrafts, and recorded all business activities in his ledger and journal.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source places bills of exchange inside the merchant finance transactions recorded by the 1430 Venetian ledger/journal system.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a common-dependency edge because bills of exchange explain the long-distance credit activity captured in the ledger, whereas Lloyd's insurance brokerage offices are later and outside the corrected Venetian merchant-ledger scope.

ontology_before: Merchant Accounting Ledgers were anchored to London insurance brokerage offices around Lloyd's, narrowing a broad merchant-ledger node to a late insurance-office context.
ontology_after: Merchant Accounting Ledgers are anchored to Renaissance Venetian multi-book merchant accounting, with bills of exchange as the relevant long-distance finance context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: merchant_accounting_ledgers no longer directly depends on insurance_brokerage_offices and now keeps bills_of_exchange_early_finance as a common dependency.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed to Lloyd's-specific insurance-broker office ledgers.
A separate insurance_office_ledger_books node is introduced and should depend directly on insurance_brokerage_offices.
A stronger source shows that Renaissance merchant ledgers required insurance brokerage offices rather than broader credit and exchange practices.
short_reason: Bills of exchange match the source-backed long-distance merchant-ledger context; insurance brokerage offices are a later Lloyd's-specific context.
